text: {"title":"Risk-Protection Behavior Gap","group_name":"risk_protection_behavior_gap","family":"behavioral_axis_contrast","summary":"Represent each student by the imbalance between health-protective routines and health-damaging exposures, distinguishing consistently fit-like profiles from mixed, buffered, or depleted risk profiles.","depends_on":[],"strategy":"Create two bounded 0-1 axes from original columns only. Protective components: sleep_amount = clip(1 - abs(sleep_duration - 7.5) / 3.5, 0, 1); sleep_quality mapped poor=0, average=0.5, good=1; movement = mean of clip((step_count - 4000) / 6000, 0, 1), clip(exercise_duration / 45, 0, 1), and physical_activity_level mapped sedentary=0, moderate=0.5, active=1; self_care = mean of clip((water_intake - 0.5) / 2.0, 0, 1) and diet_type mapped non-veg=0.35, veg=0.7, balanced=1. Harm components: stress_level mapped low=0, medium=0.5, high=1; smoking_alcohol mapped no=0, occasional=0.5, yes=1; cardio_body_load = mean of clip((heart_rate - 75) / 28, 0, 1) and clip(max(18.5 - bmi, bmi - 25) / 8, 0, 1); sleep_deficit = clip((7 - sleep_duration) / 4, 0, 1). Average observed protective components into protective_score and observed harm components into harm_score, using 0.5 only if an entire axis is missing. Emit protective_score, harm_score, protection_minus_harm, buffered_risk = min(protective_score, harm_score), unsupported_exposure = harm_score * (1 - protective_score), and a four-state ordinal quadrant using protective_score >= 0.6 and harm_score > 0.35 thresholds.","expected_signal":"The target is described as an overall health profile, and public-health/student-health sources support treating sleep, physical activity, nutrition, tobacco/alcohol, and stress as clustered but partly separable risk domains; this contrast should help balanced accuracy by making fit look like high protection with low harm, unhealthy like low protection with high harm, and at-risk like mixed or conflicting axes.","risk":"May be partially redundant with prior burden, polarity, and substance-context groups; hand-set thresholds can mis-rank diet style, high activity, or borderline BMI/heart-rate cases, and axis scores built from few observed fields may be noisy when missingness is heavy."}
